mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase borrowing costs and compress equity valuations, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are positive in >55% of months where FEDFUNDS increases, or rolling Rank IC < 0.02 over 3 years.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate crossing above 5.0% signals labor market deterioration that reduces corporate earnings expectations, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns remain positive in >60% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 5.0%, or signal loses predictive power with Rank IC < 0.03 in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates broad-based consumer demand strength that boosts corporate revenue forecasts, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns when either series declines, or combined Rank IC drops below 0.025 over rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production during a post-shock recovery phase reflects rapid capacity utilization that lifts equity valuations, yielding positive S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO acceleration fails to predict positive 5-day returns in recovery periods, or the signal reverses direction during normal expansions.

mechanism_narrative: High inflation combined with restrictive policy forces central banks to maintain elevated rates, increasing discount rates and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive forward returns occur in >50% of high-inflation/high-rate months, or interaction term loses statistical significance in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in real GDP reflects expanding economic output that supports corporate profit margins, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: GDP growth fails to predict positive 60-day returns, or Rank IC remains below 0.03 across all rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp monthly drop in total nonfarm payrolls signals sudden labor market weakness that triggers risk-off positioning, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS declines by > 50k in a month
failure_condition: S&P 500 posts positive 5-day returns in >65% of months following a payroll decline exceeding 50,000, or signal shows no predictive edge in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production alongside stable or falling interest rates indicates accommodative financing for manufacturing expansion, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined signal predicts negative returns in >55% of cases, or the interaction effect vanishes when tested against a baseline model over rolling 2-year periods.
